purpose: KG-targeted retrieval-lift determination for #145. The original open-mpm baseline (benchmark_open_mpm_ground_truth.json) found search_kg ≡ search_semantic on 18 mixed-intent queries (only 3 were KG-relevant). This query set is *specifically designed* to exercise the symbol graph — every query asks a question whose answer requires walking call / contains / inherits / impl-of edges, not raw text matching. If search_kg fails to lift Hit@K over search_semantic on THIS set, the KG signal is dead-code retrieval ornament.
schema_notes: Each query carries a `class` (kg_callers / kg_traversal / kg_impl_of / kg_neighborhood), a `mode_hint` (always 'code'), a `seed_query` (the lexical string used to anchor the KG traversal — stage-1), an optional `seed_target_file` (where stage-1 SHOULD land for the test to be meaningful), and `ground_truth_files` (the symbol-graph-connected files that a graph-aware tool should surface). The harness runs each query through search_lexical / search_semantic / search_kg / search_all using the two-stage pattern: stage-1 lexical lookup → stage-2 same-text query with the chosen lane's stage selector.

classes.kg_callers: Question: who calls function/method X? Ground truth = set of files containing call sites. search_lexical will surface the DEFINITION (and possibly some call sites that happen to mention X literally). search_semantic will surface semantically-similar code. search_kg seeded at X should surface ALL callers via the 'called_by' edge.
classes.kg_traversal: Question: what does struct/type X contain or use? Ground truth = files containing types/functions reachable from X via contains/calls edges. search_lexical finds the definition only. search_semantic finds vaguely related types. search_kg should walk outward from X.
classes.kg_impl_of: Question: what types implement trait T? Ground truth = files containing `impl T for ...`. This is the cleanest KG win shape — `impl T for X` lines have a literal lexical token (`impl T for`) but the question is structurally about the impl-of edge.
classes.kg_neighborhood: Question: what is the call-neighborhood of entry-point Y? Ground truth = files reachable in 1-2 hops from Y. Broadest possible KG win shape.

queries.text: who calls events::publish to broadcast events to the SSE bus
queries.description: events::publish has 25+ call sites across 5 files. KG seeded at events.rs should surface all five caller files. Lexical may surface a few but cannot enumerate them.

queries.text: who calls cost_usd to record per-call LLM cost
queries.description: cost_usd has one external caller (workflow/engine.rs:1315) plus internal uses in perf.rs. KG seeded at perf.rs should surface workflow/engine.rs as the caller.

queries.text: who calls atomic_write for crash-safe state persistence
queries.description: atomic_write defined in state_writer.rs, called externally from skills/registry.rs (line 415) and used in state_writer's own tests.

queries.text: who calls atomic_append_line to write session records
queries.description: atomic_append_line called from interaction_log.rs:227 and session_record.rs:118. Pure call-site retrieval.

queries.text: who calls register_tm_tools to attach trusty-memory tools to the registry
queries.description: register_tm_tools is internal to tm_tools.rs (one external sibling via register_tm_tools_for_state_dir, same file). Defines the upper bound of how cleanly KG can surface call sites when they are co-located.

queries.text: who calls spawn_subagent_and_run to run a CLI subagent
queries.description: spawn_subagent_and_run defined in subprocess.rs, called from runtime.rs.

queries.text: what does PluginManager contain and what does it use to manage MCP plugins
queries.description: Traversal from PluginManager outward — its module siblings, its consumers, the runtime that constructs it. KG should walk contains+called_by.

queries.text: what does SessionsRegistry use to load and write the on-disk session catalogue
queries.description: SessionsRegistry is consumed by runtime.rs. Traversal probe — small but real graph neighborhood.

queries.text: what does SubprocessAgentRunner use internally to spawn and capture subprocess output
queries.description: SubprocessAgentRunner is used by runtime.rs and dispatched into by ctrl/mod.rs. Traversal from a leaf struct outward to its consumers.

queries.text: what does EVENT_LINE_PREFIX get consumed by when relaying events over stderr
queries.description: EVENT_LINE_PREFIX defined in events.rs, referenced by api/server.rs:2347 in the workflow-stderr relay path.

queries.text: what types implement the AgentRunner trait
queries.description: AgentRunner is implemented by SubprocessAgentRunner (subprocess.rs), ClaudeCodeAgentRunner and DispatchingAgentRunner (claude_code_runner.rs), InProcessAgentRunner (in_process_runner.rs). Excludes test-only mocks.

queries.text: what types implement the MemoryStore trait
queries.description: MemoryStore has 5 production impls under src/memory/ plus test-only impls (excluded). Classic impl-of edge query.

queries.text: what types implement the ModelAdapter trait for different LLM providers
queries.description: All five ModelAdapter impls (Anthropic / OpenAI / Bedrock / Ollama / Generic) live in the same file as the trait definition. Tests whether KG can isolate a single file when the answer is co-located — and whether lexical/semantic are competitive here.

queries.text: what types implement the HarnessAdapter trait for external coding harnesses
queries.description: HarnessAdapter has 7 production impls each in a sibling file under src/adapters/. Strongest impl-of probe in this set — answer requires walking the impl-of edge to 7 files.

queries.text: what types implement the TicketingClient trait for ticket backends
queries.description: TicketingClient has 4 production impls (Linear / gh-cli / GitHub REST / Jira) in sibling files. Direct impl-of probe.

queries.text: what is the call neighborhood of runtime::run the main MPM entry point
queries.description: runtime::run is the top-level MPM entry. Its call-neighborhood includes main.rs (caller), api/server.rs (HTTP server bootstrap), ctrl/mod.rs (dispatch). Broad neighborhood probe.

queries.text: what is the call neighborhood of the API server search session dispatcher
queries.description: Session-running code in api/server.rs spawns into ctrl/mod.rs, publishes to events.rs, queries runtime state. Broad neighborhood probe.

queries.text: what is the call neighborhood of the llm completion dispatcher
queries.description: LLM completion dispatcher in llm/mod.rs delegates to adapter.rs and bedrock.rs. Tight neighborhood probe.
